title: Prune stale immutable ConfigMaps after deployment
summary: Addresses sourcery-ai[bot] review feedback with two targeted fixes: (1) clamps negative retain values to 0 at function entry to prevent runtime panics from misconfigured inputs, and (2) enriches the prune log entry with baseName, ownerName, and ownerUID fields for multi-tenant correlation. A new test verifies the clamping behavior with retain=-5.

tests_checklist.item: All existing prune tests still pass (13/13)
New test covers negative retain edge case with retain=-5
Test verifies all historical ConfigMaps deleted and current preserved
Test follows existing pattern: NewGomegaWithT, fake client, explicit CreationTimestamps

code_quality_checklist.item: Guard clause is minimal and placed at function entry before any use of retain
Log enrichment adds only fields already available in scope (no new computation)
No unnecessary changes beyond what the external review requested
Feature ID (CC-0077) included in guard clause comment

security_checklist.item: Input validation added for retain parameter (defensive clamping)

architecture_checklist.item: Fix is localized to the function that uses retain — no unnecessary abstraction
Clamping chosen over error return — appropriate since negative retain has a safe semantic (delete all historical)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Negative retain is normalized before reaching any index operation

defensive_checklist.item: Negative retain clamped to 0 preventing slice index panic
client.IgnoreNotFound still wraps Delete for idempotency
